title: pyrus sabrina
intro: Pyrus sabrina, also known as the European rowan or mountain ash, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its berries, which contain anthocyanins and other bioactive compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Pyrus sabrina include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiviral agent. Herbal preparations of Pyrus sabrina berries can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to promote cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
